title: Maerua socotrana
taxonomy: Maerua socotrana is classified within the family Capparaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as caperids. It is also classified in the order Brassicales, which includes other families of flowering plants such as Brassicaceae. The classification of Maerua socotrana is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids. Maerua socotrana is a species of plant that is endemic to the island of Socotra.
uses: Maerua socotrana is a plant species native to the island of Socotra, and it has been reported to have various medicinal properties. Traditionally, it has been used in local medicine to treat different health conditions. The plant's extracts have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential medicinal uses.
